seoTitle: Logic Apps vs Power Automate: What's the Relationship
metaDescription: Understand how Azure Logic Apps and Power Automate relate, where they overlap, and when to use each for your workflows.
intro: Azure Logic Apps and Power Automate share the same underlying automation engine but serve different audiences and use cases. This guide breaks down how they relate, where they differ, and how to decide which one fits your project.

steps.heading: Understand what each platform is
steps.body: Power Automate is built for business users automating everyday tasks, while Logic Apps is aimed at developers building enterprise-grade integrations on Azure.

steps.heading: Compare key similarities and differences
steps.body: Both share triggers, actions, and connectors, but differ in pricing models, scalability, and the level of technical control available.

steps.heading: Look at real-world use cases and integrations
steps.body: See how businesses use Power Automate for departmental automation and Logic Apps for large-scale, integration-heavy enterprise workflows.

steps.heading: Decide which platform fits your needs
steps.body: Choose Power Automate for quick business automations and Logic Apps when you need deeper Azure integration and enterprise scalability.

faq.question: Are Logic Apps and Power Automate the same product?
faq.answer: No, they run on the same underlying automation engine but are packaged differently for different audiences and use cases.

faq.question: Can Logic Apps and Power Automate work together?
faq.answer: Yes, businesses often use both together, combining Power Automate's ease of use with Logic Apps' enterprise integration capabilities.

faq.question: Which one is cheaper?
faq.answer: Pricing structures differ significantly, with Power Automate using per-user licensing and Logic Apps using consumption-based Azure pricing, so cost depends on your usage pattern.

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to use Logic Apps?
faq.answer: Logic Apps generally requires more technical familiarity with Azure than Power Automate, which is designed to be accessible to non-developers.
